title: unpad uses u32-to-usize cast that silently truncates on 16-bit targets
description: messages.rs:564 casts u32 original_len to usize. On 32/64-bit hosts (all current targets) this is fine, but on hypothetical 16-bit (wasm16) hosts a value > 65535 would silently truncate. The existing bounds check (4 + original_len > padded.len()) catches any exploitable case because padded.len() is also bounded, but the cast is sloppy. Use usize::try_from(original_len).map_err(|_| ...) for defense-in-depth.
